RepurpOS

Alzheimer's Disease

Disease intelligence for Alzheimer's Disease and Other Dementias: 155 alterations and 705 ranked therapeutics from curated public databases.

NIH funding$3.62BFY2025 · RCDC estimate
Funding levelModerate
US mortality142.3K deaths/yrCDC NCHS 2024
Spontaneous remissionProgressive and irreversible by definition; MMSE decline 1.07-3.4 points/year untreated; no spontaneous remission
Best-intervention remissionCholinesterase inhibitors (donepezil, rivastigmine, galantamine) slow MMSE decline to 0.2-1.37 points/year — slowing…
Gap sizeSmall for symptom slowing; biological ceiling is genuinely low with current approaches
Primary barrierbiological
Full remission profile →

Disease overview

Sources: Seeds, Open Targets, HPO, ClinicalTrials.gov, DGIdb, ChEMBL, PubMed MeSH, PubMed Literature · Pipeline phase 7 · Updated 2026-07-05

Remission & chronicity

Spontaneous remission
Progressive and irreversible by definition; MMSE decline 1.07-3.4 points/year untreated; no spontaneous remission
Best-intervention remission
Cholinesterase inhibitors (donepezil, rivastigmine, galantamine) slow MMSE decline to 0.2-1.37 points/year — slowing, not stopping; lecanemab (anti-amyloid) reduced clinical decline rate by 27% at 18 months in early AD (CLARITY AD, 2022); donanemab showed 35% slowing in amyloid/tau positive group
Drug-free remission
—
Relapse after remission
—
Chronicity
—
Gap size
Small for symptom slowing; biological ceiling is genuinely low with current approaches
Primary barrier
biological
Barrier detail: True disease reversal or cure is not achievable with any current therapy; cholinesterase inhibitors have consistent but modest effects (average 2.7-point ADAS-Cog improvement vs placebo); anti-amyloid biologics represent a genuine mechanistic step forward but are not cures and have significant side effects (ARIA — amyloid-related imaging abnormalities in 21-36%)

Last SoC change: 2023 — FDA full approval of lecanemab (Leqembi) for early AD — first disease-modifying therapy; 2003 memantine added; cholinesterase inhibitors approved 1993-2001; aducanumab (2021) withdrawn after controversy; this represents the first genuine SOC change since early 2000s

Remission data layers: disease_db_100 · source-locked manual data · confidence: high

Related: Biomarker atlas · Intervention atlas

Identifiers: MONDO: MONDO:0004975 · EFO: EFO_0000249 · OMIM: 104300 · ORPHA: ORPHA:102 · MESH: D000544 · UMLS_CUI: C0002395

Therapeutics

Direct disease associations, biomarker-linked drugs, OSMF narrative-review natural agents, merged ranking, and clinical trial / literature evidence (top agents)

DrugTypePhaseEvidence ScoreTrials & literatureSourcesLinks
INDOMETHACIN Small molecule Approved Strong 105
3 Cochrane 2 Meta-analysis 2 Trial pubs 1 RCT
View evidence

Published literature

  • Cochrane review Non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drugs as a treatment for Alzheimer's disease: a systematic review and meta-analysis of treatment effect (Drugs & aging, 2015)
  • Cochrane review Aspirin, steroidal and non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drugs for the treatment of Alzheimer's disease (The Cochrane database of systematic reviews, 2012)
  • Cochrane review Indomethacin for the treatment of Alzheimer's disease patients (The Cochrane database of systematic reviews, 2002)
  • Clinical trial publication No effect of one-year treatment with indomethacin on Alzheimer's disease progression: a randomized controlled trial (PloS one, 2008)
  • Clinical trial publication Clinical trial of indomethacin in Alzheimer's disease (Neurology, 1993)

DONEPEZIL HYDROCHLORIDE Small molecule Approved Strong 100
2 Meta-analysis 31 Trial pubs 23 RCT
View evidence

Published literature

  • Meta-analysis The role of tonifying kidney decoction and acupuncture in the treatment of Alzheimer's disease: A network meta-analysis (Medicine, 2022)
  • Meta-analysis A systematic review of the efficacy of donepezil hydrochloride combined with nimodipine on treating vascular dementia (Medicine, 2022)
  • Clinical trial publication Effect of Social Restriction Due to the COVID-19 Pandemic on Activity of Daily Living and Disease Severity of Patients With Alzheimer Disease: Sub-analysis of a Double-blinded Noninferiority Study of Donepezil Patches and Donepezil Tablets (Alzheimer disease and associated disorders, 2025)
  • Clinical trial publication Effect of Social Isolation Due to the COVID-19 Pandemic on Cognitive Dysfunction of Patients With Alzheimer Disease: Subanalysis of Double-blinded Noninferiority Study of Donepezil Patches and Donepezil Tablets (Alzheimer disease and associated disorders, 2024)
  • Clinical trial publication Clinical observation on treatment of Alzheimer's disease with Sun's scalp-abdominal acupuncture combined with donepezil hydrochloride (Zhen ci yan jiu = Acupuncture research, 2024)
  • Clinical trial publication Effects of pressing moxibustion at Baihui (GV 20) and Guanyuan (CV 4) on cognitive impairment and serum levels of Aβ(1-42), tau, P-tau in patients with mild to moderate Alzheimer's disease (Zhongguo zhen jiu = Chinese acupuncture & moxibustion, 2024)
  • Clinical trial publication Effect of acupuncture at the acupoints for Yizhi Tiaoshen on the functional connectivity between the hippocampus and the brain in the patients with Alzheimer's disease (Zhongguo zhen jiu = Chinese acupuncture & moxibustion, 2023)

CT.gov search · Cochrane · PubMed

FLORBETAPIR F 18 Small molecule Approved Strong 100
3 Trial pubs 3 RCT
View evidence

Published literature

  • Clinical trial publication TRAILBLAZER-ALZ 4: A phase 3 trial comparing donanemab with aducanumab on amyloid plaque clearance in early, symptomatic Alzheimer's disease (Alzheimer's & dementia : the journal of the Alzheimer's Association, 2025)
  • Clinical trial publication A high-glycemic diet is associated with cerebral amyloid burden in cognitively normal older adults (The American journal of clinical nutrition, 2017)
  • Clinical trial publication Physical Activity and Amyloid-β Brain Levels in Elderly Adults with Intact Cognition and Mild Cognitive Impairment (Journal of the American Geriatrics Society, 2015)

GALANTAMINE HYDROBROMIDE Small molecule Approved Strong 100
3 Trial pubs 3 RCT
View evidence

Published literature

  • Clinical trial publication A prospective, double-blind, community-controlled comparison of three doses of galantamine in the treatment of mild to moderate Alzheimer's disease in a Korean population (Clinical therapeutics, 2004)
  • Clinical trial publication The clinical efficacy and safety of galantamine in the treatment of Alzheimer's disease (CNS spectrums, 2004)
  • Clinical trial publication The cognitive benefits of galantamine are sustained for at least 36 months: a long-term extension trial (Archives of neurology, 2004)

MEMANTINE HYDROCHLORIDE Small molecule Approved Strong 100
4 Trial pubs 3 RCT
View evidence

Published literature

  • Clinical trial publication [Tolerability and efficacy of the long-term administration of memantine hydrochloride (Memary®) in patients with moderate to severe Alzheimer's disease] (Nihon Ronen Igakkai zasshi. Japanese journal of geriatrics, 2014)
  • Clinical trial publication Treatment with cholinesterase inhibitors and memantine of patients in the Alzheimer's Disease Neuroimaging Initiative (Archives of neurology, 2011)
  • Clinical trial publication Investigation of the pharmacokinetic and pharmacodynamic interactions between memantine and glyburide/metformin in healthy young subjects: a single-center, multiple-dose, open-label study (Clinical therapeutics, 2005)
  • Clinical trial publication Memantine hydrochloride: pharmacological and clinical profile (Drugs of today (Barcelona, Spain : 1998), 2004)

NICOTINE Small molecule Approved Strong 100
3 Cochrane 1 Meta-analysis 18 Trial pubs 10 RCT
View evidence

Published literature

  • Cochrane review Features and outcomes of drugs for combination therapy as multi-targets strategy to combat Alzheimer's disease (Journal of ethnopharmacology, 2018)
  • Cochrane review Efficacy and safety of nicotine on Alzheimer's disease patients (The Cochrane database of systematic reviews, 2001)
  • Cochrane review Nicotine for Alzheimer's disease (The Cochrane database of systematic reviews, 2000)
  • Meta-analysis [Smoking and neurological disorders] (Nihon rinsho. Japanese journal of clinical medicine, 2013)
  • Systematic review Smoking and increased Alzheimer's disease risk: a review of potential mechanisms (Alzheimer's & dementia : the journal of the Alzheimer's Association, 2014)
  • Clinical trial publication Radiofrequency fields, transthyretin, and Alzheimer's disease (Journal of Alzheimer's disease : JAD, 2010)
  • Clinical trial publication PET imaging of the in vivo brain acetylcholinesterase activity and nicotine binding in galantamine-treated patients with AD (Neurobiology of aging, 2008)
  • Clinical trial publication Changes in brain 11C-nicotine binding sites in patients with mild Alzheimer's disease following rivastigmine treatment as assessed by PET (Psychopharmacology, 2007)
  • Clinical trial publication PET imaging of cortical 11C-nicotine binding correlates with the cognitive function of attention in Alzheimer's disease (Psychopharmacology, 2006)
  • Clinical trial publication Nicotine and sensory memory in Alzheimer's disease: an event-related potential study (Brain and cognition, 2002)
  • Randomized controlled trial Effects of transdermal nicotine on learning, memory, verbal fluency, concentration, and general health in a healthy sample at risk for dementia (International psychogeriatrics, 2001)
  • Randomized controlled trial Effects of acute nicotine administration on cognitive event-related potentials in tacrine-treated and non-treated patients with Alzheimer's disease (Neuropsychobiology, 2002)

CT.gov search · Cochrane · PubMed

PRAZOSIN Small molecule Approved Strong 100
1 Cochrane 1 Trial pubs 1 RCT

Published literature

  • Cochrane review Risks and benefits of current and novel drugs to treat agitation in Alzheimer's disease (Expert opinion on drug safety, 2022)
  • Clinical trial publication Prazosin for the treatment of behavioral symptoms in patients with Alzheimer disease with agitation and aggression (The American journal of geriatric psychiatry : official journal of the American Association for Geriatric Psychiatry, 2009)

RIVASTIGMINE Small molecule Approved Strong 100
42 Cochrane 45 Meta-analysis 163 Trial pubs 97 RCT
View evidence

Published literature

  • Cochrane review Cost-effectiveness of traditional and novel neurotherapeutic agents for Alzheimer's disease: A systematic review (Journal of Alzheimer's disease : JAD, 2026)
  • Cochrane review Comparative safety of cholinesterase inhibitors and memantine for dementia: a protocol for a network meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials (Systematic reviews, 2025)
  • Cochrane review Rapid health technology assessment of galantamine for the treatment of Alzheimer's disease: A review (Medicine, 2025)
  • Cochrane review Effects of rivastigmine on gait in patients with neurodegenerative disorders: A systematic review and meta-analysis (PloS one, 2024)
  • Cochrane review Effect of long-term pharmacological treatments on Alzheimer disease: A systematic review and network meta-analysis (Medicine, 2024)
  • Meta-analysis Bridging the gap: Efficacy of combined therapies for cognitive, behavioral, and functional outcomes in Alzheimer's disease - results from a systematic review and network meta-analysis (Journal of Alzheimer's disease : JAD, 2025)
  • Meta-analysis Evaluating the efficacy and safety of Alzheimer's disease drugs: A meta-analysis and systematic review (Medicine, 2024)
  • Meta-analysis Safety and efficacy of acetylcholinesterase inhibitors for Alzheimer's disease: A systematic review and meta-analysis (Advances in clinical and experimental medicine : official organ Wroclaw Medical University, 2024)
  • Clinical trial publication Efficacy and safety of a novel, extended-release rivastigmine transdermal patch (TW-4752N) in patients with Alzheimer's disease: A 24-week randomized, double-blind trial with a 28-week open-label extension (Journal of Alzheimer's disease : JAD, 2026)
  • Clinical trial publication Comparative Bioavailability Study of a Novel Multi-Day Patch Formulation of Rivastigmine (Twice Weekly) with Exelon® Transdermal Patch (Daily)- A Randomized Clinical Trial (Current Alzheimer research, 2022)
  • Clinical trial publication Rivastigmine improves dual-task gait velocity in patients with Alzheimer's disease (BMC neurology, 2021)
  • Clinical trial publication Sixteen-Week Interventional Study to Evaluate the Clinical Effects and Safety of Rivastigmine Capsules in Chinese Patients with Alzheimer's Disease (Journal of Alzheimer's disease : JAD, 2019)

Published literature

  • Cochrane review Effect of long-term pharmacological treatments on Alzheimer disease: A systematic review and network meta-analysis (Medicine, 2024)
  • Cochrane review Statins for the treatment of dementia (The Cochrane database of systematic reviews, 2010)
  • Cochrane review Statins for the prevention of dementia (The Cochrane database of systematic reviews, 2009)
  • Meta-analysis The calcium-free form of atorvastatin inhibits amyloid-β(1-42) aggregation in vitro (The Journal of biological chemistry, 2022)
  • Systematic review Alzheimer's Disease: Efficacy of Mono- and Combination Therapy. A Systematic Review (Journal of geriatric psychiatry and neurology, 2022)
  • Clinical trial publication Atorvastatin May Correct Dyslipidemia in Adult Patients at Risk for Alzheimer's Disease Through an Anti-Inflammatory Pathway (CNS & neurological disorders drug targets, 2016)
  • Clinical trial publication Effects of atorvastatin on cerebral blood flow in middle-aged adults at risk for Alzheimer's disease: a pilot study (Current Alzheimer research, 2012)
  • Clinical trial publication Randomized controlled trial of atorvastatin in mild to moderate Alzheimer disease: LEADe (Neurology, 2010)
  • Clinical trial publication The Atorvastatin/Donepezil in Alzheimer's Disease Study (LEADe): design and baseline characteristics (Alzheimer's & dementia : the journal of the Alzheimer's Association, 2008)
  • Clinical trial publication Change in circulating C-reactive protein is not associated with atorvastatin treatment in Alzheimer's disease (Neurological research, 2006)

View evidence

Published literature

  • Cochrane review Statins for the prevention of dementia (The Cochrane database of systematic reviews, 2016)
  • Cochrane review Statins for the treatment of dementia (The Cochrane database of systematic reviews, 2010)
  • Cochrane review Can statins prevent or help treat Alzheimer's disease? (Journal of Alzheimer's disease : JAD, 2010)
  • Cochrane review Statins for the prevention of dementia (The Cochrane database of systematic reviews, 2009)
  • Cochrane review Statins for the prevention of Alzheimer's disease (The Cochrane database of systematic reviews, 2001)
  • Meta-analysis Evidence for benefit of statins to modify cognitive decline and risk in Alzheimer's disease (Alzheimer's research & therapy, 2017)
  • Clinical trial publication Effects of Simvastatin on Plasma Amyloid-β Transport in Patients with Hyperlipidemia: A 12-Week Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Trial (Journal of Alzheimer's disease : JAD, 2022)
  • Clinical trial publication Effect of simvastatin on CSF Alzheimer disease biomarkers in cognitively normal adults (Neurology, 2017)
  • Clinical trial publication Simvastatin ameliorate memory deficits and inflammation in clinical and mouse model of Alzheimer's disease via modulating the expression of miR-106b (Biomedicine & pharmacotherapy = Biomedecine & pharmacotherapie, 2017)
  • Clinical trial publication LDL-lipids from patients with hypercholesterolaemia and Alzheimer's disease are inflammatory to microvascular endothelial cells: mitigation by statin intervention (Clinical science (London, England : 1979), 2015)
  • Clinical trial publication Longitudinal plasma amyloid beta in Alzheimer's disease clinical trials (Alzheimer's & dementia : the journal of the Alzheimer's Association, 2015)
  • Randomized controlled trial Toward onset prevention of cognitive decline in adults with Down syndrome (the TOP-COG study): study protocol for a randomized controlled trial (Trials, 2014)

DONEPEZIL HYDROCHLORIDE Small molecule Approved Strong 100
2 Meta-analysis 31 Trial pubs 23 RCT
View evidence

Published literature

  • Meta-analysis The role of tonifying kidney decoction and acupuncture in the treatment of Alzheimer's disease: A network meta-analysis (Medicine, 2022)
  • Meta-analysis A systematic review of the efficacy of donepezil hydrochloride combined with nimodipine on treating vascular dementia (Medicine, 2022)
  • Clinical trial publication Effect of Social Restriction Due to the COVID-19 Pandemic on Activity of Daily Living and Disease Severity of Patients With Alzheimer Disease: Sub-analysis of a Double-blinded Noninferiority Study of Donepezil Patches and Donepezil Tablets (Alzheimer disease and associated disorders, 2025)
  • Clinical trial publication Effect of Social Isolation Due to the COVID-19 Pandemic on Cognitive Dysfunction of Patients With Alzheimer Disease: Subanalysis of Double-blinded Noninferiority Study of Donepezil Patches and Donepezil Tablets (Alzheimer disease and associated disorders, 2024)
  • Clinical trial publication Clinical observation on treatment of Alzheimer's disease with Sun's scalp-abdominal acupuncture combined with donepezil hydrochloride (Zhen ci yan jiu = Acupuncture research, 2024)
  • Clinical trial publication Effects of pressing moxibustion at Baihui (GV 20) and Guanyuan (CV 4) on cognitive impairment and serum levels of Aβ(1-42), tau, P-tau in patients with mild to moderate Alzheimer's disease (Zhongguo zhen jiu = Chinese acupuncture & moxibustion, 2024)
  • Clinical trial publication Effect of acupuncture at the acupoints for Yizhi Tiaoshen on the functional connectivity between the hippocampus and the brain in the patients with Alzheimer's disease (Zhongguo zhen jiu = Chinese acupuncture & moxibustion, 2023)

Published literature

  • Clinical trial publication TRAILBLAZER-ALZ 4: A phase 3 trial comparing donanemab with aducanumab on amyloid plaque clearance in early, symptomatic Alzheimer's disease (Alzheimer's & dementia : the journal of the Alzheimer's Association, 2025)
  • Clinical trial publication A high-glycemic diet is associated with cerebral amyloid burden in cognitively normal older adults (The American journal of clinical nutrition, 2017)
  • Clinical trial publication Physical Activity and Amyloid-β Brain Levels in Elderly Adults with Intact Cognition and Mild Cognitive Impairment (Journal of the American Geriatrics Society, 2015)

Published literature

  • Clinical trial publication The clinical efficacy and safety of galantamine in the treatment of Alzheimer's disease (CNS spectrums, 2004)
  • Clinical trial publication The cognitive benefits of galantamine are sustained for at least 36 months: a long-term extension trial (Archives of neurology, 2004)

MEMANTINE HYDROCHLORIDE Small molecule Approved Strong 100
4 Trial pubs 3 RCT
View evidence

Published literature

  • Clinical trial publication [Tolerability and efficacy of the long-term administration of memantine hydrochloride (Memary®) in patients with moderate to severe Alzheimer's disease] (Nihon Ronen Igakkai zasshi. Japanese journal of geriatrics, 2014)
  • Clinical trial publication Treatment with cholinesterase inhibitors and memantine of patients in the Alzheimer's Disease Neuroimaging Initiative (Archives of neurology, 2011)
  • Clinical trial publication Investigation of the pharmacokinetic and pharmacodynamic interactions between memantine and glyburide/metformin in healthy young subjects: a single-center, multiple-dose, open-label study (Clinical therapeutics, 2005)
  • Clinical trial publication Memantine hydrochloride: pharmacological and clinical profile (Drugs of today (Barcelona, Spain : 1998), 2004)

RIVASTIGMINE Small molecule Approved Strong 100
42 Cochrane 45 Meta-analysis 163 Trial pubs 97 RCT
View evidence

Published literature

  • Cochrane review Cost-effectiveness of traditional and novel neurotherapeutic agents for Alzheimer's disease: A systematic review (Journal of Alzheimer's disease : JAD, 2026)
  • Cochrane review Comparative safety of cholinesterase inhibitors and memantine for dementia: a protocol for a network meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials (Systematic reviews, 2025)
  • Cochrane review Rapid health technology assessment of galantamine for the treatment of Alzheimer's disease: A review (Medicine, 2025)
  • Cochrane review Effects of rivastigmine on gait in patients with neurodegenerative disorders: A systematic review and meta-analysis (PloS one, 2024)
  • Cochrane review Effect of long-term pharmacological treatments on Alzheimer disease: A systematic review and network meta-analysis (Medicine, 2024)
  • Meta-analysis Bridging the gap: Efficacy of combined therapies for cognitive, behavioral, and functional outcomes in Alzheimer's disease - results from a systematic review and network meta-analysis (Journal of Alzheimer's disease : JAD, 2025)
  • Meta-analysis Evaluating the efficacy and safety of Alzheimer's disease drugs: A meta-analysis and systematic review (Medicine, 2024)
  • Meta-analysis Safety and efficacy of acetylcholinesterase inhibitors for Alzheimer's disease: A systematic review and meta-analysis (Advances in clinical and experimental medicine : official organ Wroclaw Medical University, 2024)
  • Clinical trial publication Efficacy and safety of a novel, extended-release rivastigmine transdermal patch (TW-4752N) in patients with Alzheimer's disease: A 24-week randomized, double-blind trial with a 28-week open-label extension (Journal of Alzheimer's disease : JAD, 2026)
  • Clinical trial publication Comparative Bioavailability Study of a Novel Multi-Day Patch Formulation of Rivastigmine (Twice Weekly) with Exelon® Transdermal Patch (Daily)- A Randomized Clinical Trial (Current Alzheimer research, 2022)
  • Clinical trial publication Rivastigmine improves dual-task gait velocity in patients with Alzheimer's disease (BMC neurology, 2021)
  • Clinical trial publication Sixteen-Week Interventional Study to Evaluate the Clinical Effects and Safety of Rivastigmine Capsules in Chinese Patients with Alzheimer's Disease (Journal of Alzheimer's disease : JAD, 2019)

3 Cochrane 1 Meta-analysis 18 Trial pubs 10 RCT
View evidence

Published literature

  • Cochrane review Features and outcomes of drugs for combination therapy as multi-targets strategy to combat Alzheimer's disease (Journal of ethnopharmacology, 2018)
  • Cochrane review Efficacy and safety of nicotine on Alzheimer's disease patients (The Cochrane database of systematic reviews, 2001)
  • Cochrane review Nicotine for Alzheimer's disease (The Cochrane database of systematic reviews, 2000)
  • Meta-analysis [Smoking and neurological disorders] (Nihon rinsho. Japanese journal of clinical medicine, 2013)
  • Systematic review Smoking and increased Alzheimer's disease risk: a review of potential mechanisms (Alzheimer's & dementia : the journal of the Alzheimer's Association, 2014)
  • Clinical trial publication Radiofrequency fields, transthyretin, and Alzheimer's disease (Journal of Alzheimer's disease : JAD, 2010)
  • Clinical trial publication PET imaging of the in vivo brain acetylcholinesterase activity and nicotine binding in galantamine-treated patients with AD (Neurobiology of aging, 2008)
  • Clinical trial publication Changes in brain 11C-nicotine binding sites in patients with mild Alzheimer's disease following rivastigmine treatment as assessed by PET (Psychopharmacology, 2007)
  • Clinical trial publication PET imaging of cortical 11C-nicotine binding correlates with the cognitive function of attention in Alzheimer's disease (Psychopharmacology, 2006)
  • Clinical trial publication Nicotine and sensory memory in Alzheimer's disease: an event-related potential study (Brain and cognition, 2002)
  • Randomized controlled trial Effects of transdermal nicotine on learning, memory, verbal fluency, concentration, and general health in a healthy sample at risk for dementia (International psychogeriatrics, 2001)
  • Randomized controlled trial Effects of acute nicotine administration on cognitive event-related potentials in tacrine-treated and non-treated patients with Alzheimer's disease (Neuropsychobiology, 2002)

View evidence

Published literature

  • Cochrane review Effect of long-term pharmacological treatments on Alzheimer disease: A systematic review and network meta-analysis (Medicine, 2024)
  • Cochrane review Statins for the treatment of dementia (The Cochrane database of systematic reviews, 2010)
  • Cochrane review Statins for the prevention of dementia (The Cochrane database of systematic reviews, 2009)
  • Meta-analysis The calcium-free form of atorvastatin inhibits amyloid-β(1-42) aggregation in vitro (The Journal of biological chemistry, 2022)
  • Systematic review Alzheimer's Disease: Efficacy of Mono- and Combination Therapy. A Systematic Review (Journal of geriatric psychiatry and neurology, 2022)
  • Clinical trial publication Atorvastatin May Correct Dyslipidemia in Adult Patients at Risk for Alzheimer's Disease Through an Anti-Inflammatory Pathway (CNS & neurological disorders drug targets, 2016)
  • Clinical trial publication Effects of atorvastatin on cerebral blood flow in middle-aged adults at risk for Alzheimer's disease: a pilot study (Current Alzheimer research, 2012)
  • Clinical trial publication Randomized controlled trial of atorvastatin in mild to moderate Alzheimer disease: LEADe (Neurology, 2010)
  • Clinical trial publication The Atorvastatin/Donepezil in Alzheimer's Disease Study (LEADe): design and baseline characteristics (Alzheimer's & dementia : the journal of the Alzheimer's Association, 2008)
  • Clinical trial publication Change in circulating C-reactive protein is not associated with atorvastatin treatment in Alzheimer's disease (Neurological research, 2006)

3 Cochrane 1 Meta-analysis 18 Trial pubs 10 RCT
View evidence

Published literature

  • Cochrane review Features and outcomes of drugs for combination therapy as multi-targets strategy to combat Alzheimer's disease (Journal of ethnopharmacology, 2018)
  • Cochrane review Efficacy and safety of nicotine on Alzheimer's disease patients (The Cochrane database of systematic reviews, 2001)
  • Cochrane review Nicotine for Alzheimer's disease (The Cochrane database of systematic reviews, 2000)
  • Meta-analysis [Smoking and neurological disorders] (Nihon rinsho. Japanese journal of clinical medicine, 2013)
  • Systematic review Smoking and increased Alzheimer's disease risk: a review of potential mechanisms (Alzheimer's & dementia : the journal of the Alzheimer's Association, 2014)
  • Clinical trial publication Radiofrequency fields, transthyretin, and Alzheimer's disease (Journal of Alzheimer's disease : JAD, 2010)
  • Clinical trial publication PET imaging of the in vivo brain acetylcholinesterase activity and nicotine binding in galantamine-treated patients with AD (Neurobiology of aging, 2008)
  • Clinical trial publication Changes in brain 11C-nicotine binding sites in patients with mild Alzheimer's disease following rivastigmine treatment as assessed by PET (Psychopharmacology, 2007)
  • Clinical trial publication PET imaging of cortical 11C-nicotine binding correlates with the cognitive function of attention in Alzheimer's disease (Psychopharmacology, 2006)
  • Clinical trial publication Nicotine and sensory memory in Alzheimer's disease: an event-related potential study (Brain and cognition, 2002)
  • Randomized controlled trial Effects of transdermal nicotine on learning, memory, verbal fluency, concentration, and general health in a healthy sample at risk for dementia (International psychogeriatrics, 2001)
  • Randomized controlled trial Effects of acute nicotine administration on cognitive event-related potentials in tacrine-treated and non-treated patients with Alzheimer's disease (Neuropsychobiology, 2002)

SIMVASTATIN Repurposing via ABCA1 Small molecule Approved Strong 95
6 Cochrane 4 Meta-analysis 16 Trial pubs 9 RCT
View evidence

Published literature

  • Cochrane review Statins for the prevention of dementia (The Cochrane database of systematic reviews, 2016)
  • Cochrane review Statins for the treatment of dementia (The Cochrane database of systematic reviews, 2010)
  • Cochrane review Can statins prevent or help treat Alzheimer's disease? (Journal of Alzheimer's disease : JAD, 2010)
  • Cochrane review Statins for the prevention of dementia (The Cochrane database of systematic reviews, 2009)
  • Cochrane review Statins for the prevention of Alzheimer's disease (The Cochrane database of systematic reviews, 2001)
  • Meta-analysis Evidence for benefit of statins to modify cognitive decline and risk in Alzheimer's disease (Alzheimer's research & therapy, 2017)
  • Clinical trial publication Effects of Simvastatin on Plasma Amyloid-β Transport in Patients with Hyperlipidemia: A 12-Week Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Trial (Journal of Alzheimer's disease : JAD, 2022)
  • Clinical trial publication Effect of simvastatin on CSF Alzheimer disease biomarkers in cognitively normal adults (Neurology, 2017)
  • Clinical trial publication Simvastatin ameliorate memory deficits and inflammation in clinical and mouse model of Alzheimer's disease via modulating the expression of miR-106b (Biomedicine & pharmacotherapy = Biomedecine & pharmacotherapie, 2017)
  • Clinical trial publication LDL-lipids from patients with hypercholesterolaemia and Alzheimer's disease are inflammatory to microvascular endothelial cells: mitigation by statin intervention (Clinical science (London, England : 1979), 2015)
  • Clinical trial publication Longitudinal plasma amyloid beta in Alzheimer's disease clinical trials (Alzheimer's & dementia : the journal of the Alzheimer's Association, 2015)
  • Randomized controlled trial Toward onset prevention of cognitive decline in adults with Down syndrome (the TOP-COG study): study protocol for a randomized controlled trial (Trials, 2014)

Published literature

  • Cochrane review The microbiota-gut-brain axis in mild cognitive impairment and Alzheimer's disease: a scoping review of human studies (Alzheimer's & dementia : the journal of the Alzheimer's Association, 2026)
  • Cochrane review Effectiveness of probiotic supplements on cognitive function in mild cognitive impairment and Alzheimer's disease: A meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials (General hospital psychiatry, 2025)
  • Cochrane review Efficacy of probiotic supplementation in influencing cognitive function in Alzheimer's disease: A systematic review and meta-analysis (Journal of food science, 2025)
  • Cochrane review Effect of probiotics on cognitive function in adults with mild cognitive impairment or Alzheimer's disease: A meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials (Medicina clinica, 2024)
  • Cochrane review Probiotic supplements are effective in people with cognitive impairment: a meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials (Nutrition reviews, 2023)
  • Randomized controlled trial Efficacy and Safety of MT104, A Dietary Supplement Based on Cuscuta Seeds and Heat-Killed Probiotics, on Cognitive Function in Patients with Mild Cognitive Impairment: A 12-Week, Multicenter, Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Clinical Trial (Journal of medicinal food, 2025)
  • Randomized controlled trial Effect of the probiotic Escherichia coli Nissle 1917 on serum levels of NADPH oxidase-2 and lipopolysaccharide in patients with Alzheimer's disease (Journal of Alzheimer's disease : JAD, 2025)
  • Randomized controlled trial The effects of probiotic supplements on oxidative stress and inflammation in subjects with mild and moderate Alzheimer's disease: a randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled study (Inflammopharmacology, 2024)
  • Randomized controlled trial Efficacy of Probiotic Supplements on Brain-Derived Neurotrophic Factor, Inflammatory Biomarkers, Oxidative Stress and Cognitive Function in Patients with Alzheimer's Dementia: A 12-Week Randomized, Double-Blind Active-Controlled Study (Nutrients, 2023)
  • Randomized controlled trial Probiotic intervention benefits multiple neural behaviors in older adults with mild cognitive impairment (Geriatric nursing (New York, N.Y.), 2023)
